user_symptom: Customers will experience increased latency. Query (and other) jobs will timeout, or fail.
Affected customers experienced elevated error rates and latency when connecting to Google APIs.

Affected customers in Europe were unable to load the console, or experienced timeouts. Affected customers in Europe experienced unavailable errors and latency for both data and admin operations. Affected customers experienced probe creation request failures. Affected customers experienced elevated errors.

Increased error rate. Google Front End (GFE) prevented API requests from reaching the service. Cloud Firestore saw 80% of listen streams become disconnected. Cloud Monitoring had elevated error rates. Cloud Compute Engine requests saw an increase in 502 errors. Cloud Spanner experienced elevated latency spikes which may have resulted in connection timeouts. Google Cloud SQL had minor connectivity problems.

Google Cloud Monitoring experienced errors querying monitoring data. Customers may have experienced errors or incomplete monitoring data. Customers may have also experienced false alerts during the impact window based on the underlying monitoring data.
Cloud Networking traffic going through us-central1 and us-east1 shows increased latency.
APIs, Credentials, Monitoring, IAM and several other pages on the Cloud console are loading slowly and may ended up as a loading failure.
UNKNOWN
customers experiencing elevated rates of packet loss in southamerica-east1-[a, b, c]
Slow or stuck operations for zonal PD-Standard devices in us-west1-b.

Google Cloud Console - Affected customers in Europe were unable to load the console, or experienced timeouts. Container Registry - Affected customers were unable to connect to the service using the API in europe-west1. Cloud Bigtable - Affected customers in Europe experienced unavailable errors and latency for both data and admin operations. Cloud Spanner - Affected customers in Europe experienced unavailable errors and latency for both data and admin operations. Cloud Endpoints - Service was unavailable in europe-west1 and europe-west4. Cloud SQL - Affected customers experienced probe creation request failures in europe-west1, europe-west4, and europe-west5. Cloud Eventarc - Affected customers were unable to create triggers in europe-west1. App Engine - Affected customers experienced elevated errors in europe-west1.

Multiple Google Workspace and Google Cloud Platform services experienced elevated error rates. A small number of users of the following Google Workspace products experienced HTTP 500 Internal Server Errors: Calendar, Tasks, Gmail, Drive, Docs, Meet, Keep, Jamboard, and Chat. A small number of users of GCP may have experienced increased error rates and availability. Cloud Firestore customers may have experienced elevated latency, operation deadline expirations and/or error messages that state Policy Checks are unavailable.
Visible, near 100% packet loss on Cloud Interconnect.
Queries exhibited higher than normal latency. Customers were unable to fully utilize their reserved slots.
Affected customers may have experienced delayed log entries in Log Explorer or other export destinations for the duration of the incident.

Affected Customers may have observed issues with network partitions and accessing workload VMs. Affected Customers may have experienced connectivity issues using vCenter, HCX, NSX Manager, and NSX-T Edge virtual machines.
Logs-based metrics data points may be missing or delayed
Logs won't be available, high latency when reading data.
Issues with seeing monitoring metric data especially delivery_latency_health_score. Other metrics data may also be affected.
Querying for a record defined in one private zone that points to a record in another private zone does not return the final answer.
new connections to Cloud Pub/Sub experienced high latency leading to timeouts and elevated error rates for a duration of 95 minutes.
Increased TLS handshake errors or connection terminations for GCLB traffic served from HTTPS load balancers in Western Europe.
Users experiencing failure with file uploads
Customers may not be able to look at live logs by running "tail" commands

Customers using Google Cloud Networking experienced a network traffic disruption in us-central1, us-east1, us-west1 regions... Some customers using Cloud Networking experienced severe traffic disruption for the two occurrences of the incident.
GKE cluster pods restart when docker restarts.

Some services in us-east1-c and us-east1-d are failing, customers impacted by this incident would likely experience a total unavailability of zonal services hosted in us-east1-c or us-east1-d. It is possible for customers to experience service interruption in none, one, or both zones.
preventing customers from creating or updating Support cases. Cloud Console pages that are nested under https://console.cloud.google.com/support may have also failed to load or update.
Attempts to create a new instance or to delete an instance fail with an error. Instances created may experience DNS propagation delays.
This may appear as timeouts, 5XX errors and potential authorization errors being returned from the service.
GKE clusters running node pools that use docker may experience containers restarting every time docker restarts.
Customers impacted by this issue would have experienced abnormal instance terminations for instances which were running in the impacted building.

Cloud SQL External Server or Database Migration Service (DMS) users who migrate to Cloud SQL for MySQL 5.7 with GTID replication using managed dumps may experience errors if they have insert/update/delete transactions during the dump phase. Furthermore, if updates were made to those jobs during the replication phase, the migration job may fail.
Customers impacted by this issue may see high latency, packet loss, and service unavailable errors.
Connectivity issues in us-central1 resulting in failures and elevated latency.
users attempting to update or deploy Cloud Load Balancers may experience delays or failures.

Customers using CNAME chasing may experience an issue with Cloud DNS. Querying for a record defined in one private zone that points to a record in another private zone does not return the final answer.

Some services in us-east1-c and us-east1-d are failing, customers impacted by this incident would likely experience a total unavailability of zonal services hosted in us-east1-c or us-east1-d. It is possible for customers to experience service interruption in none, one, or both zones.
Customers are unable to login to their admin workstation in both their prod and non-prod environments.

Customers attempting to create github triggers using a Cloud Console URL that ends in their project id (example: https://console.cloud.google.com/cloud-build/triggers/add?project=$project-id) will see a "Failed to load" error with following message:
"There was an error while loading /cloud-build/triggers?project=
Please try again"
Customers may see GKE Cluster pods restarting when Docker restarts.
Some customers may be experiencing connection failures in Docker workflow to GCLB, GCB, or Fastly destinations such as debian.org, github.com with a timeout error.
Users might experience higher latency.
We are investigating errors with creating and deleting Cloud Composer environments.
We are investigating an issue with GCE Networking in us-east4 region affecting GCE VMs, GKE, Cloud VPN and Cloud Private Interconnect resulting in network packet loss.
Google BigQuery API returning 503 errors
Node pool creation failing in multiple zones
Networking issues in us-central1-c impacting multiple GCP products. Our engineers have applied a mitigation and the error rate is now decreasing.
We experiencing delays in showing logging.
Affected customers are unable to access all Stackdriver services via stackdriver.com, the Cloud Console, and the API.
StackDriver Trace API is experiencing an elevated rate of request failures.
UNKNOWN
UNKNOWN
Cloud SQL First Generation instances unavailable in us-central1-a
We are investigating increased packet loss into and out of southamerica-east1.
The issue with Google Kubernetes Engine Node Pool creation through the Cloud Console UI.
Google Stackdriver Alerts not firing reliably
Current data indicate that all GKE API requests in region europe-west4 are failing. GKE API unavailable in region europe-west4
Creation of new google cloud functions and update of current one are failing
UNKNOWN
We are experiencing an issue with Google Cloud Networking and Google Compute Engine in us-east4 that prevents the creation of GCE instances that require allocation of new external IP addresses.
Affected customers are unable to access all Stackdriver services via stackdriver.com, the Cloud Console, and the API.
intermittent traffic disruption to and from us-central
The Google Compute Engine service is experiencing delays in network programming in us-east1.
We are investigating timeouts with Google Cloud Functions deployments.
Users may see slow performance or intermittent errors.
Google Cloud Storage started experiencing degraded availability for some objects... error rate was ~3.5%.
GCS requests originating in Europe experienced a 17% error rate. We are currently investigating elevated latency and error rates for Google Cloud Storage traffic transiting through Europe.
Stackdriver Uptime Monitoring experienced a period of unavailability
We are experiencing an issue with packet loss for Google Cloud VPN, Cloud Interconnect, and Cloud Router beginning at Monday, 2018-10-22 13:42 US/Pacific.
UNKNOWN
We are investigating errors with GKE dashboard.
Stackdriver Logging Service Degraded
Support Center inaccessible
Support Center inaccessible
Current data indicates traffic to other regions may see approximately 12% packet loss.
UNKNOWN
Cloud Storage showing inconsistent result for object listing for multi-regional buckets in US
The rate of OAuth login errors is decreasing.
Google Cloud Engine VM instances losing connectivity
users are unable to create new support cases.
We are investigating loss for traffic to and from South America. The rate of errors is decreasing.
App Engine Admin-API experiencing high error rates
We are experiencing an issue with Cloud SQL V2, some instance maybe failing to create or HA instances may reports incorrect 'Unable to Failover' state.
Affected customers are unable to access all Stackdriver services via stackdriver.com, the Cloud Console, and the API.
UNKNOWN
You may be affected if you notice Google BigQuery jobs failing with an "Internal Error" message.
We are investigating errors with Firebase Test Lab. All Firebase Test Lab submissions will be paused and queued for later execution.
We are investigating errors with billing enabled projects causing console issues. Unable to browse GCS storage buckets in Google Console
High latency and failures on queries and deployments.

We are experiencing an issue with Google Cloud Storage service returning elevated error rates for requests in the US multi-region. Google Cloud Storage service is reporting a 1% error rate for all requests.
Cloud SQL admin operations failures, including instance creation.
UNKNOWN
UNKNOWN
UNKNOWN

We are experiencing an issue with Google Cloud Storage service returning elevated error rates for requests in the US multi-region... Google Cloud Storage service is reporting a 1% error rate for all requests. Affected Google Cloud Functions customers may seen their deployments time out. Affected customers of Google Cloud Build were observable as "Build failed (internal error)"
Datastore experiencing elevated error rates
Users are unable to create new support cases.
We are experiencing an issue with Memcache availability beginning at November 6, 2017 at 12:30 pm US/Pacific.
Elevated errors are occurring intermittently across many BQ APIs, but most common in TableService, DatasetService, and JobService operations.
UNKNOWN
Google Cloud Function deployments seeing increased errors
Customers may experience intermittent elevated errors from the BigQuery API in europe-west2.
Customers without an organization were unable to create projects through the Enablement API and received an error message.
Google Cloud VPN, Cloud Interconnect, and Cloud Router experiencing packet loss in multiple regions

Google Cloud Networking experienced loss of connectivity to external IP addresses located in us-east4 for a duration of 58 minutes. Google Compute Engine, Google Cloud VPN, and Google Cloud Network Load Balancers hosted in the us-east4 region experienced 100% packet loss from the internet and other GCP regions. Google Dedicated Interconnect Attachments located in us-east4 also experienced loss of connectivity.
Google Kubernetes Engine may clear certain add-ons from the configuration UI after a cluster upgrade.
Jobs using Dataflow Shuffle may fail or (when using Flexible Resource Scheduling) be queued indefinitely.
Issue affecting the ability to list projects and organizations
preventing the creation of new GCLB load balancers and updating of existing ones.

Customers affected by this issue may see an error similar to the following:

ERROR: (gcloud.functions.deploy) OperationError: code=3, message=Build failed: # github.com/cloudevents/sdk-go/v2/extensions

src/github.com/cloudevents/sdk-go/v2/extensions/distributed_tracing_extension.go:161:3: cannot use span (type trace.Span) as type *trace.Span in return argument:

*trace.Span is pointer to interface, not interface; Error ID: 1093f764
We are experiencing an issue with Cloud Router monitoring data (metrics) missing...Diagnosis: Visible decrease in Cloud Router data in Metrics Explorer.
UNKNOWN
Autoscaling of instances in Managed Instance Groups may not occur due to instances being unreachable.
Jobs using Dataflow Shuffle may fail or (when using Flexible Resource Scheduling) be queued indefinitely.
Customers are not able to create new databases in Cloud Firestore.
We are investigating elevated errors with Cloud Interconnect in Google Cloud Networking in us-west2. Customers peering with us-west2 may experience packet loss.
You may be affected if you notice Google BigQuery jobs failing with an "Internal Error" message.
We experiencing delays in showing logging.
Google Cloud Dataflow jobs experiencing system lag.
Affected customers may experience an elevated error rate (up to 100%) when submitting dataset insertion operations.
Google BigQuery users experiencing elevated latency and error rates in US multi-region.
Customers may experience increased latency or connection timeouts between GCP and other Cloud Providers.
Affected customers may experience timeout errors upon cluster creation and deletion.
We are experiencing an intermittent issue with Cloud Firestore
All Firebase Test Lab submissions will be paused and queued for later execution.
Cloud SQL instances in europe-west2-a may be unreachable
We are investigating an issue with Google Kubernetes Engine node pool creation through Cloud Console UI.
We are investigating an issue with Google App Engine reduced URLFetch availability starting at 17:15pm US/Pacific.
Customers may observe their calico node pods failing to start and crash-looping indefinitely on GKE 1.24.7+ and 1.25.3+.
Affected customers were unable to create new instances, as the region field in the user interface (UI) could not be populated due to a metadata issue.
Cloud Functions Gen 2 customers are missing metrics and experiencing incorrect billing for their SKUs.
100 % of customers running “yum” in RHEL instances received a 403 - Forbidden response. All Red Hat instances attempting to get updates encountered failures for the duration of the outage.
MemoryStore for Redis - instances update/export failures
Customers may have experienced up to 40% packet loss between VMs in the following affected regions.
ssh fails with ERROR: (gcloud.compute.start-iap-tunnel) Error while connecting [4047: 'Failed to lookup instance']
Calico enabled GKE clusters’ pods may get stuck Terminating or Pending after upgrading to 1.22+.
Customer can observe higher number of failures (ERROR: PERMISSION_DENIED: The caller does not have permission) when trying to list/describe the OAuth client via gCloud or Terraform
Customers may see failures while attempting to update/export their basic tier instances.

Vertex AI Online Prediction experienced increased error rates from 30% up to 100% per region. Affected customers may have experienced: all pre-existing Vertex models undeployed on Vertex AI Endpoints, empty responses when listing the deployed models, runtime exceptions and general errors on Predict and Explain requests, and quota failure when trying to re-deploy models.
Affected customers would have experienced elevated query latencies or in some cases query failures.

Affected customers experienced failures creating or editing GCE instances via the Google Cloud Console and may have received an error “Constraint constraints/compute.vmExternalIpAccess violated for project [project ID].“

Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) experienced connectivity issues between instances and/or with on-premises machines. Customers were unable to access their Cloud Volume Service (CVS) storage across GCP Regions. Customers may have faced issues with traffic routing In some cases where dynamic routes were not propagated appropriately on peered networks across regions.

Customer's might experience following:
1) External HTTP/S Load Balancing, Cloud CDN - config changes will be accepted & fail to propagate
2) Cloud Armor rules may also be affected
3) Appengine flex customers may see apps fail to deploy
4) Traffic Director may see configs fail to propagate
5) VM instance group healthchecks & functionality like autoscaling/autohealing may be affected

Customers may experience replication failure after maintenance on replica, if the master instance is busy with INSERTs/UPDATEs on tables with timestamp column and session timezone is set to other than SYSTEM.

Affected customers would have experienced increased packet loss and increased latency for the affected metros which would have caused delays and timeouts for the following services : - Cloud Load Balancing - Cloud Interconnect - Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) - Cloud NAT - Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE)
Customers may observe deployment failures and timeout errors.

Affected customers would not have been able to create new repositories in the Asia multi-region. Customers would have received a failure message of “Request contains an invalid argument.” Affected customers in the Asia multi-region were not able to create Composer environments, receiving errors indicating either an issue with AppEngine or Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) cluster creation. Affected customers would have experienced increased error rates when creating buckets in the Asia multi-region. Affected customers would have been unable to create new clusters in the affected regions.
Customers might have experienced increased errors from apps or functions that use the Secrets Manager.
Cloud Composer v2 environments autoscaling might not work. Number of Airflow workers in the Composer environment won’t change.

Affected customers would have experienced increased packet loss and increased latency for the affected metros which would have caused delays and timeouts for the following services : - Cloud Load Balancing - Cloud Interconnect - Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) - Cloud NAT - Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE)
Customer configs propagation depending on the dos_cloud configs got delayed. Some impacted customers would not be able to make Cloud Armor config changes.

Delete function operations are erroring with following message although the corresponding resources are getting deleted and customers are able to reuse the function name
Error message:
Operation Failed. Public Error Status: code: 5 message: Delete package operation for projects/[PROJECT_ID]/locations/[REGION]/repositories/gcf-artifacts/packages/[FUNCTION_ID]%2Fcustom-run has failed. Additional information: Requested entity was not found.
Increased failure rate for SQLServer instance creation
All cloud customers communicating outside the region (including to the internet) would have seen two periods of disruption, ~8m at 04:30 AM, ~10m at 04:48 AM US/Pacific

Affected customers would not have been able to create new repositories, Composer environments, buckets, or clusters in the affected regions, receiving various error messages such as “Request contains an invalid argument.”, IAM_BACKEND_INVALID_ARGUMENT, or INTERNAL_ERROR.

Google Cloud Filestore experienced an issue where ListInstances API requests failed with error code 429. This impacted read-only API access for customers globally. Pantheon, gcloud and API access (List, GetOperation, etc.) calls failed during this time.
Customers may experience latency and errors with Composer creation.

Customers may be missing data in Cloud Monitoring for the following metrics:
- bigtable.googleapis.com/cluster/cpu_load,
- bigtable.googleapis.com/replication/latency,
- bigtable.googleapis.com/replication/max_delay
Customers using the Secrets Manager are experiencing increased errors.
Retry Build operations may fail with an error and the build will not be retried
Increased failure rate for SQLServer instance creation

Customers will be getting IAM_BACKEND_INVALID_ARGUMENT in the Asian regions for Google Cloud Storage
Customers will see INTERNAL_ERROR when performing ClusterCreation (GKE Cluster Server receives invalid_argument error when creating a GCS bucket as one step of cluster creation) for Google Kubernetes Engine
Customers will see INTERNAL_ERROR Artifact Registry
Cloud Scheduler jobs are failing due to 503 error from Google Cloud Functions.

Affected customers experienced failures creating or editing GCE instances via the Google Cloud Console and may have received an error “Constraint constraints/compute.instanceExternalIpAccess violated for project [project ID].“
Customers who are deploying GCFv2 functions and who have never enabled Cloud Run API before will observe failures with deployments.

Customers may see older jobs (finished more than 180 days ago) and jobs which are currently running disappear from the Console and API output. Jobs which are currently running will see data plane errors if the control plane job resource is deleted.
Datastore customers will experience increased errors and latency.
Customers using Local SSD on VMs could see the VM terminated unexpectedly, which shows in the console as an Instance Termination by Compute Engine.
Customers might have experienced issues while creating new forwarding rules for Global Layer 7 External load balancer or edit or delete existing ones.
Customers encountered error 429 when listing Cloud Filestore instances using API, gcloud CLI, and Google Cloud Console UI.

Customers may experience elevated inter-region latency between following regions
* Traffic between asia-east1 and asia-northeast3
* Traffic between asia-east2 and asia-east1
* Traffic between asia-east2 and asia-northeast3
* Traffic between asia-southeast1 and asia-east1
* Traffic between asia-southeast2 and asia-east1
